FlexBeam — Red Light Therapy Device Documentation & Treatment Reference Source: recharge.health | Compiled for NRT-Amsterdam.nl RAG System 1. Device Specifications Parameter
Value Wavelengths
610-650nm (red, peaks at 622nm) + 800-850nm (NIR, peaks at 814nm) LED configuration
6x NIR (800-850nm) + 2x Red (610-650nm) Total optical power output
Up to 5.5W Energy density (dose)
Up to 110 mW/cm² Session programs
3 presets — 10 minute programs with auto-shutoff Battery
Lithium-ion, 5-8 sessions (50-80 min) per charge Weight
775 grams / 1.5 lbs EMF
No EMF (electromagnetic field radiation) Regulatory
FDA Cleared Cooling
Active cooling fans for maximum power LEDs 2. Mechanism of Action (Photobiomodulation) Red and near-infrared light therapy (photobiomodulation/PBM) works by stimulating the mitochondria, the
energy-producing organelles in cells. The primary mechanism: ATP Production: Light stimulates cytochrome c oxidase in the mitochondrial electron transport chain,
increasing adenosine triphosphate (ATP) production — the primary cellular energy currency. Nitric Oxide (NO) Release: RLT stimulates release of NO into blood vessels, causing vasodilation and
increased blood flow. This drains excess fluid/swelling, relieves pressure and reduces pain. Inflammation Reduction: Near-infrared light energizes the mitochondria, reducing reactive oxygen
species (ROS) and inflammasome production, thereby reducing inflammation and pain. Hormonal Regulation: Mimics natural sunshine effects — regulates serotonin levels and stimulates
beta-endorphin release (natural pain relief and mood regulation). Cellular Recovery: Enhanced ATP and blood flow accelerates the body's natural healing processes in
muscles, joints, connective tissue and skin. 3. Clinical Applications 3.1 Pain & Recovery • Acute pain relief and chronic pain management • Muscle recovery after exercise or injury • Joint pain (knees, shoulders, hips, back) • Inflammation reduction (red pad application) • Wound healing acceleration (NOT over open wounds)

• Stiffness and reduced range of motion 3.2 Neurological & Systemic • Sleep quality improvement (avoid late evening sessions if energizing effect noted) • Stress reduction — activates parasympathetic nervous system • Mood improvement — regulates serotonin, leptin, ghrelin • Gut microbiome support — NIR light promotes beneficial gut bacteria • Immune system enhancement • Athletic performance and endurance improvement 3.3 Women's Health • Menstrual pain • Fertility support • Endometriosis • PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) • Pelvic Floor Dyssynergia • Hypothyroidism • Depression and anxiety (as adjunct therapy) 4. Treatment Programs FlexBeam has 3 preset programs (10 minutes each, auto-shutoff). Programs vary depth of tissue
penetration: Program
Primary Target
Depth
Typical Use Program 1
Superficial tissue
Skin, superficial fascia
Skin conditions, surface wounds, mood Program 2
Mid-depth tissue
Muscle, subcutaneous tissue
Muscle recovery, joint pain, circulation Program 3
Deep tissue
Deep muscle, bone, organs
Deep joint issues, organ support, chronic conditions Session guidelines: Most sessions 10-30 minutes. More is NOT necessarily better — biphasic dose
response means excessive exposure can diminish effects. Use directly on skin (not over clothing). Can be
combined with other treatments. 5. Placement Guidelines for QAT Integration FlexBeam's flexible wearable design allows targeted placement. For QAT treatment integration, place on
the tissue area being addressed: Body Area

Contraindications & Safety ABSOLUTE contraindications — do not use: • Pregnancy (any area) • Active cancerous tumors (during chemo/radiotherapy) • Open wounds (hygiene — device can be used around wound) • Directly over the eyes (use goggles within 30cm) • Breast implants / breast area • Thrombo-embolism or active bleeding Use with caution: • Heart conditions — avoid placement directly over heart, shorten sessions • Metal implants — generally safe, do not exceed recommended time • Tattooed skin — dark ink absorbs energy, risk of blistering • Tanned/deep skin tones — reduce to 5 minutes initially • Light sensitivity — begin with shortened sessions • Acute systemic infections — consult healthcare provider • Children under 12 — 3-5 minutes maximum • Toddlers — 1-2 minutes maximum • Pets — 3-5 minutes maximum 7. Dosing Principles Red light therapy follows a biphasic dose response — there is an optimal dose, and both too little AND
too much reduces effectiveness: Standard session: 10 minutes per area, 1-3x daily Maximum session: 30 minutes total (can treat multiple areas) Frequency: Daily use recommended for ongoing conditions Onset of effect: Immediate to within first few days for acute issues; weeks for chronic conditions Biphasic principle: Do not exceed recommended times — more is not better Skin contact: Always directly on skin — clothing blocks light penetration 8. Integration with QAT Treatment Protocols For NRT-Amsterdam.nl treatment protocols, RLT with FlexBeam is used as an adjunct to QAT. The
following framework applies: Program selection: Match program to tissue depth targeted by QAT treatment Timing: Can be applied before (preparation) or after (recovery) QAT session

Red pad vs FlexBeam: Red pads (QAT) for energy field; FlexBeam for photobiomodulation —
complementary, not identical Inflammation cases: FlexBeam Program 2-3 on affected area + QAT red pad combination Vascular conditions (e.g. Claudicatio): FlexBeam on affected limb, Program 2 — NO on open ulcers
(Stadium IV) Documentation: Record program used, duration, placement, and patient response Source: recharge.health (FAQ, Why FlexBeam, Product pages) | Compiled by Claude for NRT-Amsterdam.nl
Medical RAG System | April 2026 | FlexBeam is not intended to diagnose, cure, or prevent specific diseases. For
treating serious medical conditions, professional medical attention should be sought.
